assessment.summary_for_seller: Workable run, but trust and next step are not landing on all key points yet.

criteria.name: Contextual first contact
criteria.short_reason: First message is too generic and misses the signal context.
criteria.evidence_quote: Hi! Mellow handles contractor payments — we take on documents, KYC, payout with audit trail on our side. What's the most pressing issue in your situation right now?
criteria.label: Contextual opening

criteria.name: Compliance and boundary of responsibility
criteria.short_reason: Seller stated Mellow's control scope without overclaiming.
criteria.evidence_quote: Hi! Mellow handles contractor payments — we take on documents, KYC, payout with audit trail on our side. What's the most pressing issue in your situation right now?
criteria.label: Scope & responsibility boundary

criteria.name: Language fit
criteria.short_reason: Language is adapted to the chosen persona.
criteria.evidence_quote: Hi! Mellow handles contractor payments — we take on documents, KYC, payout with audit trail on our side. What's the most pressing issue in your situation right now?
criteria.label: Persona language fit

criteria.name: Handling core objections
criteria.short_reason: At least two key objections addressed substantively.
criteria.evidence_quote: Hi! Mellow handles contractor payments — we take on documents, KYC, payout with audit trail on our side. What's the most pressing issue in your situation right now?

objections.objection: Ok. What exactly does Mellow provide on documents, audit trail and process discipline, and where do you avoid overclaiming?
I need to understand the grey zones — where your scope ends and ours begins. Is that documented anywhere?
Three layers: Mellow, our legal, our finance — where do the lines run? Who owns the overlap? Без overclaim.
Let's be specific: where's the overlap between your zones and our legal side? Who owns the gap? Без overclaim.

criteria.label: Core objection handling

criteria.name: Next step
criteria.short_reason: Conversation ended without a clear next step.
criteria.evidence_quote: The boundary is explicit: our scope covers the payment chain, KYC and contractor documentation. Your legal and finance teams own the strategic decisions. The overlap is mapped — not assumed.
criteria.label: Clear next step

transcript.text: Hi! Mellow handles contractor payments — we take on documents, KYC, payout with audit trail on our side. What's the most pressing issue in your situation right now?
Ok. What exactly does Mellow provide on documents, audit trail and process discipline, and where do you avoid overclaiming?
When something breaks: named owner on our side, response SLA in the contract, full incident trail documented. The contractor gets notified, you get a status update — not a problem to resolve.
I need to understand the grey zones — where your scope ends and ours begins. Is that documented anywhere?
The boundary is explicit: our scope covers the payment chain, KYC and contractor documentation. Your legal and finance teams own the strategic decisions. The overlap is mapped — not assumed.
Three layers: Mellow, our legal, our finance — where do the lines run? Who owns the overlap? Без overclaim.
